vegan-chocolate-zucchini-bread.name: Vegan Chocolate Zucchini Bread
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Grease a 9x5-inch loaf pan.
Beat cane sugar, almond milk, applesauce, coconut oil, and vanilla together in a large bowl using an electric mixer; set aside.

Whisk flour, cocoa powder, baking powder, cinnamon, salt, and baking soda together in a separate bowl; slowly add to cane sugar mixture using an electric mixer until well mixed. Stir in zucchini and ¼ cup chocolate chips.
Pour batter into the prepared bread pan; sprinkle remaining ¼ cup chocolate chips on top.

Bake in the preheated oven until top springs back when lightly pressed and a toothpick inserted into center comes out clean, 50 to 55 minutes. Cool in the pan for 10 minutes. Remove from the pan; transfer to a wire rack to cool completely before slicing.

vegan-coca-cola-cake.name: Vegan Coca-cola Cake

ingredients.item: . diamond crystal or 1 0.25 tsp. morton kosher salt
Diamond crystal or  0.5 tsp. morton kosher salt

instructions: Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to 350°. Lightly coat a 13x9" metal baking pan with nonstick vegetable oil spray and line with parchment paper, leaving overhang on long sides. Whisk together 2 cups (400 g) granulated sugar, 1¾ cups (219 g) all-purpose flour, 1 cup (84 g) Dutch-process cocoa powder, 2 tsp. Diamond Crystal or 1¼ tsp. Morton kosher salt, 1¼ tsp. baking powder, 1¼ tsp. baking soda, and 1 tsp. arrowroot in a large bowl. Add ⅔ cup vegan sour cream, ½ cup vegetable oil, and 2 tsp. vanilla extract and mix until barely combined. (Be careful not to overmix; mixture will resemble wet sand and be somewhat dry. Large pockets of unincorporated flour are okay at this point.)

Microwave 1¼ cups Coca-Cola, 1 Tbsp. fresh lemon juice, and ½ tsp. ground cardamom (if using) in a small microwave-safe bowl on high until mixture is bubbling, about 90 seconds. (Alternatively, heat in a small saucepan over medium-high until gently boiling.) Stirring gently, slowly pour into batter; stir gently until smooth. Scrape batter into prepared pan and bake until a tester inserted into the center comes out clean, 35–45 minutes. Transfer pan to a wire rack and let cake cool in pan.

Meanwhile, place 4 oz. bittersweet chocolate, chopped, or chocolate chips in a large heatproof bowl. Microwave ⅓ cup plus 1 Tbsp. Coca-Cola in a small microwave-safe bowl on high until hot but not boiling, about 1 minute; pour over chocolate and vigorously whisk until chocolate is melted and mixture is smooth and emulsified. (If chocolate is not completely melted, microwave on high in 10-second bursts, stirring after each, until completely melted.) Chill until somewhat firm (like a soft ganache), about 20 minutes.

Beat ¾ cup (1½ sticks) vegan butter, room temperature, in the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with a paddle attachment on medium speed until fluffy, about 3 minutes. Add chocolate mixture and continue to beat on medium until incorporated and smooth, about 3 minutes. Turn motor off, add 2 cups (220 g) powdered sugar and ¾ tsp. Diamond Crystal or ½ tsp. Morton kosher salt. Mix on medium speed until frosting is fluffy and smooth, about 3 minutes.
Scoop frosting on top of cake and smooth into an even layer.

vegan-raspberry-mint-milkshake.name: Vegan Raspberry Mint Milkshake
instructions: Combine raspberries, almond milk, ice cream, and mint in a blender; blend until smooth, scraping down sides of the blender as needed.
Divide milkshake between 2 medium glasses.

vegetable-souffle-argiro.name: Vegetable Souffle

ingredients.item: Finely chopped tomatoes or tomato sauce for serving
Finely chopped tomatoes or tomato sauce for serving

instructions: Rub the potatoes and the pumpkin in a thick rub.
Add the chopped onions, leeks, cheeses, ham, semolina, herbs, salt and pepper.
Beat the eggs with the cream and add them to the mixture.
Mix the ingredients very well and pour them into an oily fireproof vessel.
Pour with the olive oil.
Bake in a well preheated ocean, at 180°C, in the resistors, for 45 minutes, until it turns golden and firm.
Let it cool a little, then cut it off.
Serve with chopped tomatoes

vegetarian-bolognese-with-soy-chorizo.name: Vegetarian Bolognese With Soy Chorizo
instructions: Bring about 3 cups lightly salted water to a boil in a medium-sized pot. Add pasta and cook, stirring occasionally, until tender yet firm to the bite, about 11 minutes.

Meanwhile, heat olive oil over medium heat in a large skillet. Add marinara sauce and diced tomatoes and stir. Add soy chorizo and mix until texture is even, 3 to 5 minutes. Add oregano, cayenne pepper, and paprika; reduce heat and simmer until pasta has finished cooking, about 8 minutes more. Add basil.
Drain cooked pasta; top with sauce. Season with black pepper and salt.

vegetarian-moroccan-harira.name: Vegetarian Moroccan Harira

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Heat oil in a large pot over medium heat and cook onion until soft and translucent, about 5 minutes. Add tomatoes, chickpeas, cilantro, parsley, mint, paprika, turmeric, ginger, harissa, and saffron. Add water and cook over medium heat until flavors have combined, about 30 minutes.

Mix a few tablespoons of soup liquid with flour and cornstarch in a small bowl and return to the soup, stirring in well. Add cherry tomatoes. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer over low heat until soup thickens, about 10 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.

venezuelan-creole-pavilion-step-by-step-.name: Venezuelan Creole Pavilion: Step-by-step Recipe
venezuelan-creole-pavilion-step-by-step-.original_name: Pabellón criollo venezolano: receta paso a paso

ingredients.item: 500 gr de caraotas o frijoles negros (ya remojados)

instructions: To start with, it pits the meat into several pieces and removes excess fat. Put it in a pot with water to exceed the level of solids.
Add to the pot with 1 / 2 teaspoon coffee powder, a touch of salt and another of pepper.
Then pits 1 / 2 onion and paddle it into small cubes. Add them to the flesh as well.
Now we have to wash 5 or 6 branches of cilantro, scattle them, separate them and put them in the pot.
The following will be to heat the pot with medium heat and let the meat be cooked - covered - for 2 hours.

On the other hand, rinse the caraotes that you have previously been tipping and remove the remaining grains or residues. It puts the clear grains into a pressure pot and adds abundant water that goes beyond the amount of solids.
Peel 1 / 2 onion and paddle them into cubes, and then add them to the pot with caraotes.
Wash and scold two apes, put them in pieces and add them to the pot.
Peel two garlic teeth, ask them lightly and add them to the production.

It also adds half a teaspoon cumin coffee maker, half a teaspoon oregano coffee machine, another half a teaspoon of sugar (optional), salt and pepper to taste, as well as 3 branches of cilantro, already washed and drained.
Cover the pressure pot and cook carrot for an hour and a half.
While we cook caraotas and meat, we will prepare other ingredients and then make a frying with them. At first we'll wash up 4 sweet hijies and scold them and get his branch and cut them into pieces.
We are also going to peel an onion and mince it into small cubes.
Then she's washing a few tomatoes and pounding them up.
En el caso de los dientes de ajo, que pelarlos y luego triturarlos, o en su defecto, picarlos menudamente.
Reserva estos ingredientes, para utilizarlos posteriormente.
When the beef is ready, it drains (without pounding) and puts it onto a plate of wood, to clear or cut it. Help with two forks, or if you have to, you can also use a knife.

It then heats a frying pan with 2 or 3 tablespoons of oil (as soon as possible), at medium low heat. When the oil's hot, make a frying with the ingredients we have minced or crushed earlier. Let them cook for 5 minutes, stirring often.
After this time, remove two tablespoons of the saffrit to mix them later with the caraotes as they are ready. Just put the rest in the frying pan and put them with the minced meat.
Once the frying and meat have been well mixed, add a cup of the broth that you had previously drained. Let the meat cook, stirring often, till it has absorbed most of the liquid or evaporate.
Test the minced meat and if necessary fix the bowl with more salt, pepper or other spices. If you wish, you can also add a little more of the cooking stock. Then mix well again, cover and reserve.

In the case of caraotas, once the cooking time has been complete, we will mix the two tablespoons of cold we have previously booked. Then we'll have them tested to atone for that and if necessary we'll have more salt, pepper or other spices.
We will mix well again and let them cook for another 15 minutes with the padded pot.
Once the cooking time's up, we'll try to check that the caraotes are soft. If that's not the case, we'll let them cook some more. Then we'll cover them and save them.

vintage-fresh-green-bean-casserole-circa.name: Vintage Fresh Green Bean Casserole (circa 1956)
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C).
Heat a large saucepan of salted water to a boil; add green beans and cook just until slightly tender, about 5 minutes. Drain.
Mix green beans and cream of mushroom soup together in a bowl; spread into a 9x13-inch baking dish. Sprinkle Cheddar cheese over the green bean mixture and top with French-fried onions.
Bake in the preheated oven until cheese is melted and browned, about 30 minutes.
